records.ety: [F. spcifique, or NL. spesificus; L. species a particular sort or kind + facere to make. Cf. specify.]

defs: Of or pertaining to a species; characterizing or constituting a species; possessing the peculiar property or properties of a thing which constitute its species, and distinguish it from other things; as, the specific form of an animal or a plant; the specific qualities of a drug; the specific distinction between virtue and vice.
quotes: Specific difference is that primary attribute which distinguishes each species from one another.

defs: Specifying; definite, or making definite; limited; precise; discriminating; as, a specific statement.
Exerting a peculiar influence over any part of the body; preventing or curing disease by a peculiar adaptation, and not on general principles; as, quinine is a specific medicine in cases of malaria.
quotes: In fact, all medicines will be found specific in the perfection of the science.

defs: A specific remedy. See Specific, a., 3.
quotes: His parents were weak enough to believe that the royal touch was a specific for this malady.

defs: Anything having peculiar adaption to the purpose to which it is applied.
